blocks:
Ayah

ٱلَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا۟ وَصَدُّوا۟ عَن سَبِيلِ ٱللَّهِ أَضَلَّ أَعْمَـٰلَهُمْ (۱)
Those who are [themselves] faithless and bar [others] from the way of Allah—He has rendered their works fruitless. (1)

Key Points

Among the one hundred and fourteen Surahs of the Holy Quran, in eleven Surahs, the first Ayah is about a threat or reproach, and except for Surah Al-Ahzab, the rest concern the enemies of Islam. 1. Surah Al-An'am. «... ثمّ الّذین كفروا بربّهم یعدلون»... But the disbelievers ascribe partners to their Lord. 2. Surah At-Tawbah. «برائة من اللّه و رسوله» Allah and His Messenger are free from the disbelievers. 3. Surah Al-Ahzab. «یا أیّها النبىّ اتّق اللّه و لاتطع الكافرین و المنافقین» O Prophet! Fear Allah and do not obey the disbelievers and the hypocrites. 4. Surah Muhammad. «الّذین كفروا و صدّوا عن سبیل اللّه» 5. Surah Al-Mumtahanah. «یا ایّها الّذین آمنوا لاتتّخذوا عدوّى و عدوّكم اولیاء» O believers! Do not take My enemy and your enemy as guardians. 6. Surah Al-Munafiqun. «اذا جاءك المنافقون...» When the hypocrites come to you... 7. Surah Al-Ma'arij. «سئل سائل بعذاب واقع» One of the hypocrites and enemies requested punishment. 8. Surah Al-Bayyinah. «لم یكن الّذین كفروا من اهل الكتاب والمشركین منفكّین حتّى تأتیهم البیّنة» The disbelievers among the People of the Book and the polytheists will not cease from their disbelief and polytheism until the clear evidence comes to them. 9. Surah Al-Fil. «ألم تر كیف فعل ربّك باصحاب الفیل» Did you not see how your Lord dealt with the People of the Elephant? 10. Surah Al-Kafirun. «قل یا أیّها الكافرون» O Prophet! Say to the disbelievers. 11. Surah Al-Masad. «تبّت یدا أبى لهب و تب» May the hands of Abu Lahab be ruined.

The phrase «صدّوا عن سبیل اللّه» can be interpreted in two ways: A) Meaning turning away, i.e., they themselves turned away from the path of Allah. B) Meaning hindering, i.e., they prevented people from the path of Allah.(3)

The phrase «أضلّ أعمالهم» can also be interpreted in two ways: A. Allah invalidates and nullifies the good deeds of the disbelievers and polytheists, such as charity and spending on the poor, due to their disbelief and their misleading of people, such that in the Hereafter, there will be no trace of these things in their records. B. Allah renders their efforts to destroy Islam and defeat the Muslims ineffective so that they do not achieve their goal and objective.

The deeds of the disbelievers are all wasted. «أضلّ أعمالهم» This wastefulness of the disbelievers' deeds is mentioned in the Holy Quran using several expressions: - «كرماد اشتدّت به الریح فى یوم عاصف»(4), the deeds of the disbelievers are like ashes upon which the wind blows fiercely on a stormy day. - «فحبطت اعمالهم»(5) The deeds of the disbelievers are wiped out. - «كسراب بقیعة»(6) The deeds of the disbelievers are like a mirage in a plain, which the thirsty mistake for water.

3) Tafsir Makhzan al-Irfan. 4) Ibrahim, 18. 5) Kahf, 105. 6) Nur, 39.

Messages

  1. In the psychology of propagation (Da'wah), sometimes one must speak without introduction. «بسم اللّه الرحمن الرحیم الّذین كفروا...»

  2. Usually, every deviation leads to another deviation; some first commit disbelief and then prevent others from believing. «كفروا و صدوا»

  3. The efforts of the disbelievers will fail; their endeavors have no lasting consequence, and people will realize the truth sooner or later. «صدوا... أضل أعمالهم»

  4. The path of Allah has enemies, and the enemies do not remain idle; they seek to mislead others. «صدّوا عن سبیل اللّه»

  5. The wasting of deeds is a type of Divine punishment for the disbelievers. «كفروا... اضلّ»

Explanations

| Portrait of Surah Muhammad صلى الله عليه وآله This Surah has thirty-eight Ayahs and was revealed in Medina. The name of the Surah is derived from its second Ayah, in which the name of the beloved Prophet of Islam, Muhammad صلى الله عليه وآله, is mentioned. War and Jihad against the enemies of Islam is the most important topic discussed in this Surah, and thus some have named it the Surah of "Qital" (Fighting). The main contents of the Surah concern the believers and the disbelievers, comparing the status of these two groups in this world and the Hereafter, and looking at the actions of the hypocrites who were engaged in destructive activities in Medina. Furthermore, issues surrounding the captivity and release of prisoners of war, the call to spend (in charity), and the prohibition of inappropriate compromise and appeasement with the enemy are discussed therein. We read in a Hadith: Whoever wishes to observe the state of us, the Ahl al-Bayt, and our enemies, should recite Surah Muhammad.(1) It is also narrated that the Holy Prophet used to recite this Surah during the Maghrib prayer.(2)

1) Tafsir Majma' al-Bayan. 2) Tafsir Maraghi.

Ayah

وَٱلَّذِينَ ءَامَنُوا۟ وَعَمِلُوا۟ ٱلصَّـٰلِحَـٰتِ وَءَامَنُوا۟ بِمَا نُزِّلَ عَلَىٰ مُحَمَّدٍ وَهُوَ ٱلْحَقُّ مِن رَّبِّهِمْ ۙ كَفَّرَ عَنْهُمْ سَيِّـَٔاتِهِمْ وَأَصْلَحَ بَالَهُمْ (۲)
But those who have faith and do righteous deeds and believe in what has been sent down to Muhammad—and it is the truth from their Lord—He shall absolve them of their misdeeds and set right their affairs. (2)

Key Points

The word bāl (بال) either means work, states, and affairs (shū'ūn) or it means spirit and heart. The repetition of the phrase āmanū (آمنوا - "they believed") indicates that faith in revelation and the commands of the Prophet must be equal in weight to faith in Allah. Furthermore, correct knowledge of Allah (God-consciousness), a sound worldview, and good behavior alone are not salvific; rather, faith in the prophethood of the Prophet of Islam and His (Allah's) Sharia (law) is also necessary. The intent of ‘amilū al-ṣāliḥāt (عملوا الصّالحات - "they did righteous deeds") is not the performance of all righteous deeds, because performing all of them is impossible for man. Nor is the intent that all the deeds of the believers are righteous, because immediately following it, He says: kuffira ‘anhum sayyi’ātihim (كفّر عنهم سیئاتهم - "He will remove their evil deeds"), meaning We compensate for their bad deeds. (7) Rather, the intent is the performance of good deeds in every field, to the extent of one's ability and possibility. The intent of mā nuzzila ‘alā Muḥammad (ما نزّل على محمّد - "that which was sent down upon Muhammad") is the Divine revelation, which includes both the Qur’an and the Sunnah (tradition) of the Noble Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله. As we read in the Qur’an: mā yanṭiqu ‘ani al-hawā (ما ینطق عن الهوى - "He does not speak out of [personal] desire"). (8) His words are not based on whim or desire. Just as the duty of prayer (ṣalāh) and fasting (ṣawm) was revealed to the Prophet, the command to appoint a successor was also revealed during Ḥajjat al-Wadā‘ (the Farewell Pilgrimage). Yā ayyuhā al-rasūlu balligh mā unzila ilayka... (یا أیّها الرّسول بلّغ ما أنزل الیك... - "O Messenger, convey that which has been sent down to you...") (9) Therefore, the glad tidings in this Ayah are specific to those who have faith in everything that was revealed to the Prophet (not just a part of it).

7) Tafsir Atyab al-Bayan. 8) Tahrim, 3. 9) Ma’idah, 67.

Messages

1- Faith has degrees and stages, and it is something capable of perfection. āmanū... wa āmanū

2- Faith and action are inseparable from one another. āmanū wa ‘amilū al-ṣāliḥāt wa āmanū

3- Faith is valuable when it leads to complete obedience to Divine commands. bimā nuzzila ‘alā Muḥammad

4- Following the commands of the Prophets is based on the standard and criterion of truth. wa huwa al-ḥaqq

5- The rulings and Sharia of the religion are for the growth and training of man. nuzzila... min Rabbihim (In other words, Divine Lordship (Rubūbiyyah) necessitates that a true program, free from deviation, be sent down for the growth and training of His servants.)

6- The criterion for truth and reality is conformity with revelation and the commands of Allah. huwa al-ḥaqqu min Rabbihim

7- The Prophet of Islam does not speak on His own accord; what He says is revelation. nuzzila ‘alā Muḥammad... min Rabbihim

8- To receive Divine favors, faith alone is not sufficient; rather, righteous action and following the Messenger of Allah are also necessary. āmanū wa ‘amilū al-ṣāliḥāt wa āmanū bimā nuzzila ‘alā Muḥammad... kuffira ‘anhum... wa aṣlaḥa bālahum

9- Faith and righteous action compensate for the past and insure the future. kuffira... aṣlaḥa

10- Disbelief (kufr) causes the ruin of deeds, while faith and righteous action cause the rectification of affairs. Alladhīna kafarū... aḍalla a‘mālahum – Alladhīna āmanū wa ‘amilū al-ṣāliḥāt... aṣlaḥa bālahum

11- Sins and evil deeds prevent man's rectification; they must first be removed so that the ground for rectification is prepared. kuffira ‘anhum sayyi’ātihim wa aṣlaḥa bālahum

12- Allah consoles the Prophet and the people of faith by threatening the disbelievers (aḍalla a‘mālahum) and giving glad tidings to the believers (aṣlaḥa bālahum).

Ayah

ذَٰلِكَ بِأَنَّ ٱلَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا۟ ٱتَّبَعُوا۟ ٱلْبَـٰطِلَ وَأَنَّ ٱلَّذِينَ ءَامَنُوا۟ ٱتَّبَعُوا۟ ٱلْحَقَّ مِن رَّبِّهِمْ ۚ كَذَٰلِكَ يَضْرِبُ ٱللَّهُ لِلنَّاسِ أَمْثَـٰلَهُمْ (۳)
That is because the faithless follow falsehood, and the faithful follow the truth from their Lord. That is how Allah draws comparisons for mankind. (3)

Key Points

Comparing and distinguishing between the ultimate fate of the disbelievers and the believers is one of the educational methods of the Quran, which has been repeatedly stated in these few verses. In this verse, faith and disbelief are separated from each other based on truth and falsehood, which demonstrates the logic of the Quran in dealing with opponents.

Messages

1- The good or bad destiny and outcome of a person is dependent on his thoughts and deeds. «أضلّ أعمالهم... أصلح بالهم - ذلك بانّ الّذین...»

2- Inclination towards falsehood is the basis for inclination towards disbelief. «الّذین كفروا اتّبعوا الباطل»

3- Any path that stands opposite to the truth is false and baseless, and there is no third way between truth and falsehood. «اتّبعوا الباطل... اتّبعوا الحق»

4- Falsehood originates from man and has no relation to God, but the truth is from Allah, and heavenly realities are not vain. «الحقّ من ربّهم»

5- The clear and manifest example of the truth is the Quran. «اتّبعوا الحقّ من ربّهم» (We read in the previous verse: «ما نزّل على محمّد و هوالحقّ من ربّهم»)

6- Using examples and stating the factors of human felicity and wretchedness in the form of parables is among the educational principles of the Quran. «ذلك بانّ... كذلك یضرب اللّه للناس امثالهم»

Ayah

فَإِذَا لَقِيتُمُ ٱلَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا۟ فَضَرْبَ ٱلرِّقَابِ حَتَّىٰٓ إِذَآ أَثْخَنتُمُوهُمْ فَشُدُّوا۟ ٱلْوَثَاقَ فَإِمَّا مَنًّۢا بَعْدُ وَإِمَّا فِدَآءً حَتَّىٰ تَضَعَ ٱلْحَرْبُ أَوْزَارَهَا ۚ ذَٰلِكَ وَلَوْ يَشَآءُ ٱللَّهُ لَٱنتَصَرَ مِنْهُمْ وَلَـٰكِن لِّيَبْلُوَا۟ بَعْضَكُم بِبَعْضٍ ۗ وَٱلَّذِينَ قُتِلُوا۟ فِى سَبِيلِ ٱللَّهِ فَلَن يُضِلَّ أَعْمَـٰلَهُمْ (٤)
When you meet the faithless in battle, strike their necks. When you have thoroughly decimated them, bind the captives firmly. Thereafter either oblige them [by setting them free] or take ransom, until the war lays down its burdens. That [is Allah’s ordinance]. Had Allah wished He could have taken vengeance on them, but that He may test some of you by means of others. As for those who were slain in the way of Allah, He will not let their works go fruitless. (4)

Key Points

«أثخنتموهم» (Athkhantumūhum) is derived from «ثخن» (thukhn), meaning complete dominance and immobilizing the enemy. «وثاق» (Wathāq) is a means of binding, such as a rope.

The philosophy of Jihad in Islam involves several matters: A) For self-defense or the defense of other oppressed people. «اُذن للذّین یقاتلون بانهم ظلموا» (10), «كما یقاتلونكم كافّة» (11) B) For the purpose of eradicating corruption and discord (fitnah). «قاتلوهم حتّى لاتكون فتنة» (12) C) To protect centers of monotheism. «لو لادفع اللّه النّاس بعضهم ببعض لهدّمت صوامع و بیع» (13) D) To defend divine laws and boundaries. «قاتلوا الّذین لایؤمنون باللّه و لا بالیوم الاخر و لا یحرّمون ما حرّم اللّه و رسوله و لایدینون دین الحق» (14)

This Ayah relates to the battlefield; meaning, on the scene of war where the disbelievers stand against you and intend to eliminate you, you must fight with decisiveness and firmness and kill them. After achieving complete dominance over them and ensuring the enemy forces are broken, take captives. After the war ends, the Islamic leader may free the captives, as he deems appropriate, either in exchange for compensation or without it. Therefore, some who have assumed that the Ayah means: "Kill any disbeliever wherever you find him," have failed to pay attention to the word «حرب» (harb), meaning war, and the issue of captivity, which arises during war.

The Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله was asked who the martyr (shahīd) in the way of Allah is. Is it someone who fought and lost his life motivated by showing off his bravery, or due to family and tribal prejudices, or out of hypocrisy and ostentation? He (the Prophet) said: «مَن قاتل لتكون كلمة اللّه هى العلیا فهو فى سبیل اللّه» (15) The martyr is one who fought so that the Word of Allah may be the highest.

10) Hajj, 39. 11) Tawbah, 36. 12) Baqarah, 193. 13) Hajj, 40. 14) Tawbah, 29. 15) Tafsir Furqan.

Messages

1- Eliminate only those disbelievers who stand against you in war. «فاذا لقیتم»

2- The Muslim combatant must be prepared, trained, and brave. «فضرب الرقاب»

3- The main target when attacking the enemy should be key and sensitive points. «الرقاب»

4- Do not think about taking captives or collecting spoils until the enemy is defeated and immobilized. «حتّى اذا اثخنتموهم»

5- Be thorough and firm during battle so that the enemy cannot take advantage and the captives do not escape. «فشدّوا الوثاق»

6- Authority must be delegated to the commander on the front line. «فاِمّا منّاً بعد و اِمّا فداءً»

7- The goal of Jihad is to prevent oppression or to eliminate tyranny, and thus pardoning and forgiving captives is among the recommendations of Islam. «فاِمّا منّاً بعد و اِمّا فداءً»

8- In Islamic Jihad, both power is necessary («فضرب الرقاب») and compassion and kindness («فاِمّا منّاً بعد و اِمّا فداء»).

9- Freeing captives without compensation (as a favor) takes precedence over freeing them with compensation. «فاِمّا منّا بعد و اِمّا فداء»

10- War and Jihad are among the contexts for divine testing. «لیبلوا بعضكم ببعض»

11- Jihad in Islam is for the preservation of God's religion, not for conquest, colonialism, threat, rivalry, revenge, envy, or other motives. «فى سبیل اللّه»

12- Although some are martyred in war, Allah brings their goals to fruition, and their efforts are never wasted. «قتلوا فى سبیل اللّه فلن یضلّ أعمالهم»

13- War is the arena of divine testing, the disbelievers are the means of testing, and the martyrs are the victors of this test. «لیبلوَا بعضكم ببعض و الّذین قتلوا... فلن یضل أعمالهم»

Ayahs

سَيَهْدِيهِمْ وَيُصْلِحُ بَالَهُمْ (٥)
He will guide them and set right their affairs, (5)
وَيُدْخِلُهُمُ ٱلْجَنَّةَ عَرَّفَهَا لَهُمْ (٦)
and admit them into paradise, with which He has acquainted them. (6)

Messages

1- Divine guidance is continuous and persists even after death and martyrdom. «قتلوا... سیهدیهم»

2- When going to the battlefield and engaging in Jihad, do not worry about your individual and family life. «یصلح بالهم»

3- Martyrdom in the way of Allah compensates for all human shortcomings; slips are forgiven, and faults are overlooked. «یصلح بالهم»

4- The Holy Essence of Allah takes responsibility for managing the martyr's affairs. «سیهدیهم... یصلح...»

5- Great encouragements and rewards are the best means for creating motivation in difficult tasks. «فلن یضلّ اعمالهم... سیهدیهم... یدخلهم الجنّة»

6- Prior knowledge of the reward increases a person's pleasure and eagerness upon receiving it. «الجنّة عرّفها لهم»

Ayah

يَـٰٓأَيُّهَا ٱلَّذِينَ ءَامَنُوٓا۟ إِن تَنصُرُوا۟ ٱللَّهَ يَنصُرْكُمْ وَيُثَبِّتْ أَقْدَامَكُمْ (۷)
O you who have faith! If you help Allah, He will help you and make your feet steady. (7)

Key Points

Man is a limited being, and his help and assistance on the path of God are also limited; but Allah is infinite, and His help arises from unlimited power. Allah has made several things incumbent upon Himself in exchange for several other things: - His attention and remembrance in exchange for man's remembrance. «فاذكرونى اذكركم»(16) - His victory and assistance in exchange for man's assistance. «ان تنصروا اللّه ینصركم» - Blessing and increase in exchange for man's gratitude. «لئن شكرتم لازیدنّكم»(17) - His fulfillment of the covenant in exchange for man's faithfulness.(18) «اوفوا بعهدى اوف بعهدكم»(19)

Imam Ali عليه السلام says: If you help Allah, He will help you and make your steps firm... Allah does not seek help and assistance from you out of humiliation or necessity... He has asked for your help while He Himself is the Almighty, the Wise, and the hosts of the heavens and the earth are under His command. The Divine request for help and loan is so that He may test you and distinguish the best doer of good deeds.(20)

16) Al-Baqarah, 152. 17) Ibrahim, 7. 18) Tafsir Kashf al-Asrar. 19) Al-Baqarah, 40. 20) Nahj al-Balaghah, Sermon 183.

Messages

1- The prerequisite of faith is practical action toward the propagation and expansion of the Divine religion. «یا ایها الّذین آمنوا ان تنصروا اللّه...»

2- Jihad against warring disbelievers is one instance of assisting the religion of Allah. «فضرب الرقاب... ان تنصروا اللّه...»

3- Divine reward and punishment are commensurate with human performance. «تنصروا اللّه ینصركم»

4- Whoever assists the religion of Islam with his thought, tongue, and action, Allah will also support him. «ان تنصروا اللّه ینصركم»

5- Receiving Divine favors depends on human effort. «ان تنصروا اللّه ینصركم» (Expecting Allah's help without effort and striving is an undue expectation.)

6- Among the Divine aids, steadfastness is the most prominent. (The mention of steadfastness comes after Divine help.) «ینصركم و یثبّت اقدامكم»

7- Divine rewards exceed our performance. «ان تنصروا اللّه ینصركم و یثبّت اقدامكم»

Ayahs

وَٱلَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا۟ فَتَعْسًا لَّهُمْ وَأَضَلَّ أَعْمَـٰلَهُمْ (۸)
As for the faithless, their lot will be to fall [into ruin], and He will render their works fruitless. (8)
ذَٰلِكَ بِأَنَّهُمْ كَرِهُوا۟ مَآ أَنزَلَ ٱللَّهُ فَأَحْبَطَ أَعْمَـٰلَهُمْ (۹)
That is because they loathed what Allah has sent down, so He made their works fail. (9)

Key Points

The word ta'sa (تعس) means slipping, falling, and stumbling face-first. One of the best methods of education is the method of comparison. In the previous verses, concerning the believing martyrs, He said: «فلن یضلّ اعمالهم» (He will never let their deeds go astray). In this verse, concerning the disbelievers, He says: «أضلّ اعمالهم» (He has made their deeds astray). This comparison is good news for the striving believers. Similarly, concerning the believers, He said: «یثبّت اقدامكم» (He will make your steps firm); Allah makes their steps firm. And this verse says concerning the disbelievers: «فتعساً لهم» (Woe to them); they fall into slippery places and precipices.

Messages

  1. Cursing stubborn disbelievers is permissible. «فتعساً لهم...» (Just as He says in another verse: «تّبت یدا أبى لهب»).

  2. Disbelief leads to ruin and destruction. «كفروا... أضلّ أعمالهم»

  3. Disliking divine commands is a sign of a type of disbelief within a person. «والّذین كفروا... كرهوا ما أنزل اللّه»

  4. Divine punishments are commensurate with a person's disbelief and dislike. «كفروا... ذلك بانهم...»

  5. Disliking the Quran and turning away from it causes good deeds to be nullified and ruined. «كرهوا... فاحبط اعمالهم»

  6. Motivation and enthusiasm give value to deeds. A person who is dissatisfied with the commands of Allah, his deeds have no value. «كرهوا... فاحبط اعمالهم»

Ayah

۞ أَفَلَمْ يَسِيرُوا۟ فِى ٱلْأَرْضِ فَيَنظُرُوا۟ كَيْفَ كَانَ عَـٰقِبَةُ ٱلَّذِينَ مِن قَبْلِهِمْ ۚ دَمَّرَ ٱللَّهُ عَلَيْهِمْ ۖ وَلِلْكَـٰفِرِينَ أَمْثَـٰلُهَا (۱٠)
Have they not travelled through the land so that they may observe how was the fate of those who were before them? Allah destroyed them, and a similar [fate] awaits these faithless. (10)

Key Points

Dammar (دَمّر) is derived from Tadmir (تدمیر), meaning destruction, annihilation, and overturning. When it is accompanied by ‘ala (على), it signifies intensity. In the Quran, the destruction and annihilation of people are based on their actions and character: - A group was destroyed due to transgression. «أهلكوا بالطّاغیه»(21) - Some were destroyed due to obstinacy and denial. «تدمّر كلّ شى ء بامر ربّها... اذ كانوا یجحدون»(22) - A number were destroyed because of plotting and deceit. «فانظر كیف كان عاقبة مكرهم انّا دمّرناهم و قومهم اجمعین»(23) - A group was destroyed because of sin. «فاهلكناهم بذنوبهم»(24) - Some were destroyed because of oppression and tyranny. «اهلكناهم لمّا ظلموا»(25)

21) Al-Haqqah, 5. 22) Al-Ahqaf, 25. 23) An-Naml, 51. 24) Al-Anfal, 54. 25) Al-Kahf, 59.

Messages

1- Negligent individuals who do not learn lessons from the destruction of past generations are subject to reproach and blame. «أفلم یسیروا»

2- Travel, tourism, and exploration must be purposeful, aimed at studying the history of nations, and serve as a factor for learning lessons and growth. «أفلم یسیروا... فینظروا»

3- Studying history and recognizing the destiny of nations plays a role in a person's worldview and faith. «افلم یسیروا... فینظروا»

4- Manifestations of luxury are not important; the end and ultimate consequence of the matter are important. «كیف كان عاقبة»

5- The actions of Allah are wise and governed by law. «دمّر اللّه... و للكافرین امثالها»

6- The Will of Allah is dominant and sovereign over history and tyrants. «دمّر اللّه...»

7- Divine wrath is not limited to one or a few instances; He can manifest His wrath in various forms. «للكافرین أمثالها»

Ayah

ذَٰلِكَ بِأَنَّ ٱللَّهَ مَوْلَى ٱلَّذِينَ ءَامَنُوا۟ وَأَنَّ ٱلْكَـٰفِرِينَ لَا مَوْلَىٰ لَهُمْ (۱۱)
That is because Allah is the protector of the faithful, and because the faithless have no protector. (11)

Key Points

The meaning of Mawla (مولى) is friend and helper (or protector); otherwise, all existence is under the guardianship (Wilayah) of Allah, and in this regard, there is no difference between the believer and the disbeliever. Believers who were seemingly without help or support in this world, and whose reliance was solely upon Allah, on the Day of Resurrection, Allah will be their Helper. «اللّه مولَى الّذین آمنوا» Just as it is in this world: «اللّه ولىّ الّذین آمنوا»(26). But the disbelievers who had friends and helpers in this world, «والّذین كفروا اولیائهم الطاغوت»(27), it will become clear on the Day of Resurrection that these are not their true helpers. «و انّ الكافرین لامولى لهم»

26) Baqarah, 256. 27) Baqarah, 256.

Messages

1- Faith is the means of attracting Divine support, and disbelief is the cause of losing it. «اللّه مولى الّذین آمنوا و انّ الكافرین لامولى لهم»

2- On the Day of Resurrection, none of the supporters of the disbelievers and polytheists will be effective. «و انّ الكافرین لامولى لهم»

Ayah

إِنَّ ٱللَّهَ يُدْخِلُ ٱلَّذِينَ ءَامَنُوا۟ وَعَمِلُوا۟ ٱلصَّـٰلِحَـٰتِ جَنَّـٰتٍ تَجْرِى مِن تَحْتِهَا ٱلْأَنْهَـٰرُ ۖ وَٱلَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا۟ يَتَمَتَّعُونَ وَيَأْكُلُونَ كَمَا تَأْكُلُ ٱلْأَنْعَـٰمُ وَٱلنَّارُ مَثْوًى لَّهُمْ (۱۲)
Indeed Allah will admit those who have faith and do righteous deeds into gardens with streams running in them. As for the faithless, they enjoy and eat like the cattle do, and the Fire will be their [final] abode. (12)

Key Points

In this Ayah, the eating of the disbelievers (kuffar) is likened to the eating of livestock (quadrupeds), and this similarity can be due to several aspects, including: A) The entire focus (himmah) of animals is reaching food. As Imam Ali عليه السلام says: «همّها علفها»(28) (Their concern is their fodder.) The sole concern of animals is reaching food. Likewise, the disbelievers have no desire except enjoyment. «ولم یرد الاّ الحیاة الدنیا»(29) B) Livestock are heedless (ghafil), and the disbelievers are also heedless of the Day of Judgment and responsibility. «أولئك كالانعام بل هم أضل أولئك هم الغافلون»(30) C) Animals do not know the source of their food, whether it is lawful (halal) or unlawful (haram). Some humans also do not consider halal and haram, and according to the Quran, as soon as they acquire an inheritance or wealth without effort, they dispose of it without regard for the rights of others. «و تأكلون التراث أكلاً لمّا»(31) Imam Ali عليه السلام says: The worth of a person who places all his effort into his stomach is the same as that which comes out of it.(32)

28) Nahj al-Balaghah, Letter 45. 29) An-Najm, 29. 30) Al-A'raf, 179. 31) Al-Fajr, 19. 32) Ghurar al-Hikam.

Messages

1- Admitting the believers into Paradise is a manifestation of Divine Guardianship (Wilayah). «بانّ اللّه مولى الّذین آمنوا... ان اللّه یدخل»

2- The people of faith and righteous deeds are in a position where Allah Himself is responsible for admitting them into Paradise. «ان اللّه یدخل...»

3- The criterion is the outcome of the matter; otherwise, material enjoyments are available to everyone, more or less. «جنّات تجرى... یأكل كما تأكل الانعام»

4- To encourage people toward the truth, utilize their natural inclinations. (Man naturally enjoys gardens and rivers.) «جنّات تجرى»

5- Enjoyment and gratification are permissible within the framework of Divine regulations. (What the Quran criticizes is animalistic gratification.) «یأكلون كما تأكل الانعام»

6- Gluttony (stomach-worship) is the most prominent of human carnal desires, because status, wealth, and lust come to a person occasionally, but the stomach is constantly demanding. (The Quran mentions eating separately alongside the title of enjoyment.) «یتمتّعون و یأكلون»

7- The life of the disbelievers is animalistic in nature, and its end and outcome are Hellish. «كما تأكل الانعام و النار مثوى لهم»

8- In evaluations, also take the final outcome into account. «النار مثوى لهم»

Ayah

وَكَأَيِّن مِّن قَرْيَةٍ هِىَ أَشَدُّ قُوَّةً مِّن قَرْيَتِكَ ٱلَّتِىٓ أَخْرَجَتْكَ أَهْلَكْنَـٰهُمْ فَلَا نَاصِرَ لَهُمْ (۱۳)
How many a town We have destroyed which was more powerful than your town which expelled you, and they had no helper. (13)

Key Points

In addition to hearing accusations such as poet, soothsayer, sorcerer, and madman, the Prophets were also threatened with various dangers, such as: A) Murder. «انّ الملاء یأتمرون بك لیقتلوك»(33) The assembly is consulting to kill you. B) Torture. «لتكوننّ من المرجومین»(34) They told Noah: You will surely be among those who are stoned. C) Exile and expulsion. «لنخرجنّك یا شعیب»(35) O Shu'ayb! We will surely expel you. D) Imprisonment. «لاجعلنّك من المسجونین»(36) O Moses! We will surely make you among the imprisoned. This Ayah reassures the Prophet that We destroyed all those previous nations, even though they were much stronger than the people of Mecca. Although the Prophet of Islam migrated by his own will, because the disbelievers of Mecca created difficult conditions for him, which led to the Prophet's decision to migrate, Allah therefore attributes the Prophet's departure to the people of Mecca. «اخرجتك»

33) Al-Qasas, 20. 34) Ash-Shu'ara, 116. 35) Al-A'raf, 88. 36) Ash-Shu'ara, 29.

Messages

1- Familiarity with history is a motivation for resistance against hardships. «و كایّن»

2- The oppressive nations that were destroyed throughout history are numerous. «و كایّن من قریة... أهلكناهم»

3- Do not fear non-Divine powers, for they are nothing compared to Divine power. «اشدّ قوّة من قریتك... أهلكناهم»

4- Disbelievers sometimes face worldly punishment, «أهلكناهم», in addition to the punishment of the Hereafter, «النّار مثوىً لهم».

5- The ultimate fate of oppressive superpowers is destruction and annihilation. «أشدّ قوّة... أهلكناهم...»

6- How often pressures and hardships become factors for movement and blessing. «اخرجتك...» (Although Allah criticized the reaction of the people of Mecca to the Prophet's call, which caused the Prophet's migration, this migration brought blessings.)

7- Reformers and religious preachers must be fully prepared for any kind of harassment and persecution from opponents. «اخرجتك»

8- Against Divine wrath, there is no helper. «فلاناصر لهم»

Ayah

أَفَمَن كَانَ عَلَىٰ بَيِّنَةٍ مِّن رَّبِّهِۦ كَمَن زُيِّنَ لَهُۥ سُوٓءُ عَمَلِهِۦ وَٱتَّبَعُوٓا۟ أَهْوَآءَهُم (۱٤)
Is he who stands on a manifest proof from his Lord like those to whom the evil of their conduct is made to seem decorous and who follow their desires? (14)

Key Points

| The term Bayyinah (clear proof) originally refers to something that creates a distance and separation between two things, such that they no longer have any connection or link to each other. For this reason, a clear and manifest proof that separates truth (Haqq) from falsehood (Batil) is called Bayyinah (37). The Prophet comes to the people with logic and Bayyinah, but the people confront him by considering their own path good and following their desires. The mission of the Prophet of Islam is accompanied by Bayyinah. He is commissioned by Allah and speaks nothing of His own accord, emphasizing this matter: «انّى على بیّنة من ربّى» (38).

37) Tafsir Namuneh. 38) An'am, 57.

Messages

1- The support of the believer is evidence and proof, «بیّنة من ربّه» (a clear proof from his Lord), but the disbelievers rely on their own assumptions and desires. «زیّن له سوء عمله» (the evil of his deeds is made fair-seeming to him).

2- Self-centeredness is the opposite of God-centeredness. Whoever does not show humility before evidence and reasoning will follow his own desires and the desires of others. «بیّنة من ربّه... اتّبعوا اهوائهم» (a clear proof from his Lord... they followed their desires).

3- Man is instinctively opposed to evil, and whenever he intends to commit a wicked act, he first gives it a beautiful coloring and veneer. «زیّن له سوء عمله» (the evil of his deeds is made fair-seeming to him).

4- The requirement of Lordship (Rububiyyah) is the presentation of clear proofs. «بیّنة من ربّه» (a clear proof from his Lord).

5- Some deeds possess inherent ugliness (qubh dhati). That is, their essence and reality do not change, even if beautification and deception take place. «زیّن له سوء عمله» (the evil of his deeds is made fair-seeming to him).

6- In order to achieve carnal desires, man justifies wicked deeds in such a way that performing them appears beautiful and desirable. «زیّن له سوء عمله و اتبعوا اهوائهم» (the evil of his deeds is made fair-seeming to him and they followed their desires).

Ayah

مَّثَلُ ٱلْجَنَّةِ ٱلَّتِى وُعِدَ ٱلْمُتَّقُونَ ۖ فِيهَآ أَنْهَـٰرٌ مِّن مَّآءٍ غَيْرِ ءَاسِنٍ وَأَنْهَـٰرٌ مِّن لَّبَنٍ لَّمْ يَتَغَيَّرْ طَعْمُهُۥ وَأَنْهَـٰرٌ مِّنْ خَمْرٍ لَّذَّةٍ لِّلشَّـٰرِبِينَ وَأَنْهَـٰرٌ مِّنْ عَسَلٍ مُّصَفًّى ۖ وَلَهُمْ فِيهَا مِن كُلِّ ٱلثَّمَرَٰتِ وَمَغْفِرَةٌ مِّن رَّبِّهِمْ ۖ كَمَنْ هُوَ خَـٰلِدٌ فِى ٱلنَّارِ وَسُقُوا۟ مَآءً حَمِيمًا فَقَطَّعَ أَمْعَآءَهُمْ (۱٥)
A description of the paradise promised to the Godwary: therein are streams of unstaling water and streams of milk unchanging in flavour, and streams of wine delicious to the drinkers, and streams of purified honey; there will be every kind of fruit in it for them, and forgiveness from their Lord. [Are such ones] like those who abide in the Fire and are given to drink boiling water which cuts up their bowels? (15)

Key Points

What was briefly mentioned regarding Paradise in Ayah 12, "جنّات تجرى" (Gardens beneath which rivers flow), is detailed in this Ayah. "فیها أنهار... و أنهار... و أنهار" (In it are rivers... and rivers... and rivers). The types of rivers in Paradise are mentioned in this Ayah, but for a specific group in Paradise, there is a special spring. "عیناً یشرب بها المقرّبون" (A spring from which those nearest [to Allah] drink) (39). A spring from which only the Muqarrabūn (those nearest [to Allah]) drink. Everything has a perfection: the perfection of water is having no odor; the perfection of milk is that its taste does not change; the perfection of drinks is being delicious; and the perfection of honey is being pure. Considering the repetition of the word "انهار" (rivers), it can be understood that each of the milk, honey, wine, and water has several rivers. Imam Husayn عليه السلام said: The forgiveness that the people of Paradise receive therein is the best blessing for them, and then He cited Ayah 72 of Sūrat al-Tawbah (40): "و رضوان من اللّه اكبر" (But the pleasure of Allah is greater). It is possible that the intent of forgiveness in Paradise is the removal of the memory of past slips from the minds of the inhabitants of Paradise, so that they do not recall their worldly mistakes and their hearts are not saddened (41).

39) مطفّفین، 28. 40) بحار، ج 8، ص 140. 41) تفسیر راهنما.

Messages

  1. In contrast to worldly displays and adornments that lead to the commission of certain sins, invite people to the truth by describing the displays, beauties, and enjoyments of Paradise. "مَثَل الجنّة الّتى وعد المتّقون..."
  2. Those who, due to piety (taqwā), refrain from fleeting and unlawful enjoyments will attain lasting enjoyment. "وعد المتقون"
  3. Giving hope for a good outcome is a factor in inclining towards truth and piety. "وعد المتقون"
  4. The desire for multiplicity and variety is a natural human demand. "انهارٌ... انهارٌ"
  5. In Paradise, alongside material and physical blessings, there are also spiritual blessings. "انهارٌ... مغفرة..."
  6. The blessings of Paradise are far removed from any defect, flaw, or change. "غیر آسن... لم یتغیّر طعمه... مصفّى"
  7. The climate of Paradise does not cause the blessings to change, become poisoned, or smell foul. "لم یتغیّر..."
  8. The blessings that the inhabitants of Paradise receive are under the shadow of the forgiveness of their Lord. "مغفرة من ربّهم"
  9. In Paradise, all kinds of fruits exist in abundance. "كلّ الثمرات"
  10. In Hell, boiling water is forced upon the disbelievers with compulsion and humiliation. "سقوا"
  11. Encouragement and threat, side by side, are constructive. "انهارٌ... ماء حمیماً"
  12. The Resurrection is physical. (Milk and honey are signs of physical enjoyment, just as the cutting up of the disbelievers' intestines indicates this.) "فقطّع امعائهم"
  13. The reckoning of the human body and physique on the Day of Judgment differs from their body and physique in this world.

Ayah

وَمِنْهُم مَّن يَسْتَمِعُ إِلَيْكَ حَتَّىٰٓ إِذَا خَرَجُوا۟ مِنْ عِندِكَ قَالُوا۟ لِلَّذِينَ أُوتُوا۟ ٱلْعِلْمَ مَاذَا قَالَ ءَانِفًا ۚ أُو۟لَـٰٓئِكَ ٱلَّذِينَ طَبَعَ ٱللَّهُ عَلَىٰ قُلُوبِهِمْ وَٱتَّبَعُوٓا۟ أَهْوَآءَهُمْ (۱٦)
There are some among them who prick up their ears at you. But when they go out from your presence, they say to those who have been given knowledge, ‘What did he say just now?’ They are the ones on whose hearts Allah has set a seal, and they follow their own desires. (16)

Key Points

After describing the situation of the believers and the disbelievers, this Ayah refers to the manner in which the hypocrites dealt with the Prophet's speech, which originated from heavenly revelation, and addresses the Prophet, saying: The disbelievers who are outwardly Muslim attend your gatherings and and listen to the Quran, but because they do not believe in your truthfulness and the truthfulness of your Book, they are unable to comprehend and accept it. Therefore, whenever they meet your followers who possess knowledge regarding your words, they say with contempt and mockery: What kind of speech is this man saying?! But O Prophet! Do not imagine that your words are incomprehensible; rather, their spirit of hostility and obstinacy has caused Allah to seal their hearts, preventing them from having the power to grasp the truth, and making them merely follow their own whims and desires. From the fact that the phrase «و اتّبعوا اهوائهم» (and they followed their desires) comes after the expression «طبع اللّه على قلوبهم» (Allah has set a seal upon their hearts), it can be inferred that if a person abandons his understanding, insight, and intellect, he falls under the command of whims, desires, and lust.(42)

(42) Tafsir Hidayat.

Messages

1- The presence of every segment of society, even disbelievers and hypocrites, was permitted in the presence of the Prophet of Islam صلى الله علیه وآله. «منهم مَن یستمع الیك»

2- It is necessary for people to recognize and expose the secretive movements of the opponents. «و منهم مَن یستمع الیك...»

3- Hypocrites seek to sow doubt and division among the elite (the knowledgeable) among the people of truth. «یستمع... قالوا للذّین اوتوا العلم»

4- Questioning is reprehensible if it is based on destruction, weakening, undermining faith, or contempt and ridicule. «ماذا قال آنفاً»

5- Divine revelation and the words of the Prophet صلى الله علیه وآله were scientific (knowledge-based) and nurtured scholars. «اوتوا العلم»

6- Sowing doubt among Muslims is a form of cultural invasion. «ماذا قال آنفاً»

7- Through corrupt motivation, action, and desire-worship, man prepares the ground for his heart to be sealed, losing the capacity to understand revelation and divine knowledge. «أولئك الّذین طبع اللّه»

8- We should not be deceived by the multitude of attendees and listeners, for perhaps they are not receptive to the truth. «و منهم مَن یستمع الیك... طبع اللّه على قلوبهم»

9- Mocking the revelation and the Quran causes the heart to become darkened and prevents man from grasping the truths. «ماذا قال آنفاً... طبع اللّه على قلوبهم»

10- If a heart is sealed, the words of the Messenger of Allah will also be ineffective in it. «طبع اللّه»

11- The human heart and soul are inherently receptive to the truth (fitrah). Certain factors cause it to be closed and sealed. (The word «طبع» is usually used in cases where the path was initially open and then closed.)

12- Whoever acts to undermine the Prophet has, in reality, taken sides against Allah. «یستمع الیك... ماذا قال آنفا... طبع اللّه على قلوبهم»

13- The baseless whims and desires of man are numerous. «اتبعوا اهوائهم»

14- Desire-worship is incompatible with the worship of Allah. «طبع اللّه على قلوبهم و اتبعوا اهوائهم»

Ayah

وَٱلَّذِينَ ٱهْتَدَوْا۟ زَادَهُمْ هُدًى وَءَاتَىٰهُمْ تَقْوَىٰهُمْ (۱۷)
As for those who are [rightly] guided, He enhances their guidance and invests them with their Godwariness. (17)

Key Points

In the previous Ayah, we read that although a group heard the words of truth and the verses of the Quran from the Prophet, they were unwilling to accept them. This Ayah states that another group, upon hearing those same verses and words, grows and achieves spiritual and moral expansion. Phrases like «زادهم هُدى» (He increased them in guidance) and «زدنى علماً» (Increase me in knowledge) (43) do not merely express an increase in guidance or knowledge; rather, they indicate the spiritual growth, flourishing, and increased capacity of the person who benefits from this guidance or knowledge. Several things cause the guidance of the believers to increase: a) The verses and teachings that Allah reveals. b) The words and wisdom of the Prophet. c) Their patience and endurance in the face of the mockery of the hypocrites. (44)

43) Taha, 114. 44) Tafsir Safi.

Messages

1- Acceptance of guidance by man paves the way for an increase in guidance from Allah. «اهتدوا زادهم»

2- Guidance has stages and levels. «زادهم هدى»

3- Accepting guidance expands man's spiritual capacity. «زادهم» (And He did not say: "He increased their guidance.")

4- One step taken by man is followed by several steps taken by Allah. «اهتدوا» is from man, «زادهم... و آتاهم» is from Allah.

5- True guidance is that which leads man to piety (Taqwa). «اهتدوا... آتاهم تقواهم»

6- Piety (Taqwa) is a divine gift and reward for those who accept guidance. «آتاهم تقواهم»

Ayah

فَهَلْ يَنظُرُونَ إِلَّا ٱلسَّاعَةَ أَن تَأْتِيَهُم بَغْتَةً ۖ فَقَدْ جَآءَ أَشْرَاطُهَا ۚ فَأَنَّىٰ لَهُمْ إِذَا جَآءَتْهُمْ ذِكْرَىٰهُمْ (۱۸)
Do they await anything except that the Hour should overtake them suddenly? Its portents have already come. When it overtakes them of what avail will the admonitions they were given? (18)

Key Points

Ashrāṭ (أشراط) is the plural of sharaṭ (شَرَط), meaning sign or mark. The intent of "فقد جاء اشراطها" (its signs have already come) in this Ayah, based on a narration from the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله, is His own mission (Bi'that).(45) This is because the final Messenger and the Divine Book have been revealed to humanity, and Allah's proof upon the people has been completed. Thus, any sin, corruption, disbelief (kufr), or denial brings the appearance of the Hour closer. Although the word al-Sā'ah (الساعة) is usually used for the Day of Judgment (Qiyamah), there is no impediment to it referring to the time when Divine wrath descends and people are destroyed in this very world, which is a prelude to the punishment of the Hour. Ibn Abbas narrates that the Noble Messenger صلى الله عليه وآله, during Hajjat al-Wada' (the Farewell Pilgrimage), while Salman al-Farsi was the closest person to Him, said: Shall I inform you of the signs of the Hour? Then He said: «اضاعة الصّلوات و اتّباع الشّهوات و المیل الى الاهواء و تعظیم أصحاب المال و بیع الدین بالدنیا»(46) (meaning) neglecting prayers, following desires, respecting the wealthy, and selling religion for the world. In some narrations, more than one hundred signs have been enumerated for the establishment of the Hour.(47)

45) Tafsir Majma' al-Bayan. 46) Tafsir Nur al-Thaqalayn. 47) Tafsir Burhan.

Messages

1- Hard-hearted disbelievers, even after witnessing all the proofs, still do not believe and await the passing of the final deadline. «فهل ینظرون الا السّاعة»

2- The establishment of the Hour and the destruction of the disbelievers is sudden and surprising. «بغتة»

3- The Hour has signs and preliminaries. «فقد جاء اشراطها»

4- Do not think that the path of repentance is always open and that there is always an opportunity for return. «فانّى لهم اذا جائتهم ذكراهم»

Ayah

فَٱعْلَمْ أَنَّهُۥ لَآ إِلَـٰهَ إِلَّا ٱللَّهُ وَٱسْتَغْفِرْ لِذَنۢبِكَ وَلِلْمُؤْمِنِينَ وَٱلْمُؤْمِنَـٰتِ ۗ وَٱللَّهُ يَعْلَمُ مُتَقَلَّبَكُمْ وَمَثْوَىٰكُمْ (۱۹)
Know that there is no god except Allah, and plead [to Allah] for forgiveness of your sin and for the faithful, men and women. Allah knows your itinerary and your [final] abode. (19)

Key Points

Mutqallab (متقلَّب) is a masdar mimi (verbal noun beginning with mim) meaning revolution, transfer, and displacement, and mathwa (مثوى) is also a masdar mimi meaning place of settlement and tranquility.

In Ayah 55 of Surah Ghafir, the same phrase, «واستغفر لذنبك» (And ask forgiveness for your sin), appears. Considering that all Prophets are infallible (ma'sum) and far removed from sin, this istighfar (seeking forgiveness) and asking for pardon is for one of the following matters:

  1. Istighfar is an act of worship, even if no sin has been committed.
  2. Istighfar for Tark al-Awla (abandoning the better course of action), meaning deeds that were not sins, but which it would have been better for a person like the Prophet not to perform.
  3. So that seeking forgiveness for sin becomes a tradition and method for the people.
  4. Istighfar causes the elevation of the Prophet's ranks.
  5. The intent is seeking forgiveness for the sins that people have committed against the Prophet.
  6. Istighfar is not always due to negligence (taqsir), but sometimes due to shortcoming (qusur). When a servant observes the might and majesty of Allah and his own lowliness and humility, he seeks istighfar and asks for pardon.(48)

Some commentators have said that dhanb (ذَنب) does not always mean disobedience and sin, but rather means the "tail of the action" (dhanab fi'l) and the consequences of actions and behavior. According to this meaning, the Prophet's dhanb is the consequences of the Prophet's invitation toward Allah, which involves receiving harm and evil from opponents, such as desecration, murder, and harassment. The heavier the divine call, the more difficult its dhanb (consequences) will be, requiring more istighfar. Seeking forgiveness for it means asking the Almighty God to protect us from the evil of the opponents.(49)

In the Quran, istighfar is also narrated from other Prophets, such as David, Moses, and Abraham علیهم السلام. The istighfar of great figures for the general public has also been mentioned repeatedly in the Quran, such as the istighfar of Prophet Abraham for his parents and the believers(50) and even his uncle,(51) the istighfar of Prophet Jacob for his sons,(52) the istighfar of Prophet Moses for his brother,(53) and the istighfar of the angels for the people.(54) And in this Ayah, the istighfar of the Prophet of Islam صلى الله علیه وآله for the believing men and believing women, which is itself a type of intercession (shafa'ah).

48) Tafsirs Majma' al-Bayan, Kabir Fakhr Razi, and Al-Mizan. 49) Tafsir Furqan. 50) Ibrahim, 41. 51) Shu'ara, 86. 52) Yusuf, 98. 53) A'raf, 151. 54) Shura, 5 and Ghafir, 7.

Messages

1- Now that you have seen that We reward the believers and punish the disbelievers, know that Allah is the ultimate authority. «فاعلم» (Know, therefore)

2- Faith and Monotheism must be based on awareness. «فاعلم انه لا اله الا اللّه» (Imitation/blind following in the fundamentals of belief is not permissible.)

3- In the divine system, the friends of Allah (Awliya) are intermediaries for the people to receive divine grace and forgiveness. Although Allah is capable of forgiving people without the Prophet's prayer, He commands the Prophet to pray and seek istighfar. «استغفر... للمؤمنین» (Ask forgiveness... for the believing men)

4- A leader must be compassionate toward the people. «استغفر... للمؤمنین و المؤمنات»

5- Both women and men are included in the Prophet's prayer, and the criterion for the Prophet's prayer is being a believer, not gender. «للمؤمنین والمؤمنات»

6- Do not rely solely on the Prophet's istighfar; rather, you yourselves should consider Allah present and feel shame for sinning in His presence. «واللّه یعلم متقلّبكم و مثواكم» (And Allah knows your moving about and your resting place)

7- Self-reform must precede the reform of others. «و استغفر لذنبك و للمؤمنین...»

8- All people, even the divine Messengers, are in need of Allah's grace. «و استغفر لذنبك و للمؤمنین...» (The majesty is the characteristic of the Creator, the weakness and smallness belong to the creation, and the request is for overlooking shortcomings.)

9- Allah desires pardon and forgiveness for the people, and thus He commands His Prophet to seek istighfar for the people and act as an intermediary. «و استغفر لذنبك و للمؤمنین...»

10- Allah's comprehensive knowledge of all human conditions causes concern over the punishment for slips and leads to turning toward istighfar. «استغفر... اللّه یعلم متقلّبكم و مثواكم»

11- Allah's knowledge has no limits, and nothing escapes His sight. (Whether we are moving or stationary makes no difference to Him.) «یعلم متقلّبكم و مثواكم»

Ayah

وَيَقُولُ ٱلَّذِينَ ءَامَنُوا۟ لَوْلَا نُزِّلَتْ سُورَةٌ ۖ فَإِذَآ أُنزِلَتْ سُورَةٌ مُّحْكَمَةٌ وَذُكِرَ فِيهَا ٱلْقِتَالُ ۙ رَأَيْتَ ٱلَّذِينَ فِى قُلُوبِهِم مَّرَضٌ يَنظُرُونَ إِلَيْكَ نَظَرَ ٱلْمَغْشِىِّ عَلَيْهِ مِنَ ٱلْمَوْتِ ۖ فَأَوْلَىٰ لَهُمْ (۲٠)
The faithful say, ‘If only a surah were sent down!’ But when a conclusive surah is sent down and war is mentioned in it, you see those in whose hearts is a sickness looking upon you with the look of someone fainting at death. So woe to them! (20)

Key Points

The word "Sūrah" is a Quranic term that encompasses a collection of verses (āyāt); it was not coined later. The meaning of "Sūratun Muḥkamah" (a firm/clear chapter) is a Sūrah whose verses are firm and clear, not ambiguous or vague (mutashābih). The meaning of those "in whose hearts is a disease" (fī qulūbihim maraḍun) in this verse does not refer to the hypocrites (munāfiqūn), because hypocrites are not counted among the believers, and the verse is about the believers.(55) Therefore, sometimes faith (īmān) exists, but the heart does not possess complete soundness.

55) Tafsīr al-Mīzān.

Messages

1- A group of believers is awaiting the command for Jihād (struggle), and they constantly express this readiness. «یقول الّذین آمنوا...»

2- Believers base their way of life in all dimensions upon Divine instructions. «یقول الّذین آمنوا لو لانزّلت سورة»

3- The command for Jihād must be based on revelation (waḥy) and Divine instructions. «لو لانزلت سورة»

4- Jihād is required only where the reasons for its necessity are firm and clear. «سورة محكمة»

5- War and Jihād are not the primary goal of Divine commands, but rather one of the ordinances (aḥkām). «ذكر فیها القتال»

6- The command for Jihād is a field for testing individuals. «ذكر فیها القتال رایت الّذین...»

7- Individuals can be evaluated based on the type of their gaze, view, and perspective. «رایت... ینظرون الیك»

8- Fear of Jihād is a sign of a diseased heart, and those weak in faith see their own death with their eyes upon the command for Jihād, just as a person sees death during the throes of death (iḥtiḍār). «فى قلوبهم مرض ینظرون الیك نظر المغشىّ علیه من الموت»

9- The slogan of "woe" (or "death") and dissociation (tabarrī) from others has a Quranic root. «فاولى لهم»

Ayah

طَاعَةٌ وَقَوْلٌ مَّعْرُوفٌ ۚ فَإِذَا عَزَمَ ٱلْأَمْرُ فَلَوْ صَدَقُوا۟ ٱللَّهَ لَكَانَ خَيْرًا لَّهُمْ (۲۱)
Obedience and upright speech.... So when the matter has been resolved upon [concerning going to war], if they remain true to Allah that will surely be better for them. (21)

Key Points

The meaning of ṭāʿah (طاعة) is complete, unified obedience. The phrase ʿazm al-amr (عزم الامر) means the emergence of a situation that requires decisive resolve and will, and some have interpreted it to mean the intensification and severity of war. Weak-willed individuals, due to fear of Jihad, resort to justification, excuse-making, and irrelevant talk, in addition to disobedience and shirking the duty of Jihad. The Quran refers to their excuses in several verses, including their saying that: - We do not have the strength or ability to fight. «لاطاقة لنا» (56) - Our families are not safe, and our homes are exposed and unprotected. «بیوتنا عورة» (57) - The weather is hot. «لاتنفروا فى الحَرّ» (58) - If we participate, we will commit sin. During the Battle of Tabuk, they made the excuse that looking at the Roman girls would cause temptation (fitnah), so they would not go to the front. «و لاتفتنّى». But the Quran considered this very flight from battle to be the temptation. «ألا فى الفتنة سقطوا» (59) - Now is not the right time for war; it is better to postpone it. «لولا اخّرتنا» (60) - We fear that misfortune or harm will befall us. «نخشى أن تُصیبنا دائرة» (61) In this verse, He says: If, at the time of the command for Jihad, instead of these kinds of excuses, you obey and speak a good word, it is better. «طاعة و قول معروف»

56) Al-Baqarah, 249. 57) Al-Ahzab, 13. 58) At-Tawbah, 81. 59) At-Tawbah, 49. 60) An-Nisa, 77. 61) Al-Ma'idah, 52.

Messages

1- The believer is completely obedient in the face of Allah’s command for Jihad. «طاعة»

2- Both speech and action must be correct and appropriate. «طاعة و قول معروف»

3- Speak kindly and appropriately regarding divine commands. «و قول معروف»

4- Jihad and defense are commendable according to reason and innate nature (fiṭrah). «قول معروف»

5- The root of the fear of Jihad is that we do not act truthfully (sincerely). «فلو صدقوا اللّه»

6- Allah’s command for Jihad is for the benefit of man (not God). «لكان خیراً لهم»

Ayahs

فَهَلْ عَسَيْتُمْ إِن تَوَلَّيْتُمْ أَن تُفْسِدُوا۟ فِى ٱلْأَرْضِ وَتُقَطِّعُوٓا۟ أَرْحَامَكُمْ (۲۲)
May it not be that if you were to wield authority you would cause corruption in the land and ill-treat your blood relations? (22)
أُو۟لَـٰٓئِكَ ٱلَّذِينَ لَعَنَهُمُ ٱللَّهُ فَأَصَمَّهُمْ وَأَعْمَىٰٓ أَبْصَـٰرَهُمْ (۲۳)
They are the ones whom Allah has cursed, so He made them deaf, and blinded their sight. (23)

Key Points

Commentators have interpreted "تَولّیتم" (tawallaytum) in two ways: A) Turning away and abandoning the front (of battle), becoming preoccupied with wrongdoing, and severing kinship ties (silat al-rahm). B) Taking authority over the people and then suppressing and destroying others. However, the second meaning is not consistent with the context of the verses.(62) The Ayah addresses individuals of weak faith who turn away from participating in Jihad to save their lives, stating: Do not imagine that by abandoning Jihad you will remain safe, because by fleeing the battle, you create the conditions for the spread of corruption (fasad) and murder, which will also affect you, making even the killing of children permissible for you, just as it was during the Age of Ignorance (Jahiliyyah).

62) Tafsir al-Mizan.

Messages

1- Turning away from religion and the commands of the Quran paves the way for corruption and ruin. «اِن تولّیتم... تفسدوا فى الارض و...»

2- Abandoning kinship ties (silat al-rahm) and family relations is a sin on par with spreading corruption on earth. «تفسدوا فى الارض و تقطّعوا ارحامكم»

3- If corruption spreads, a person will not even show mercy to his own relatives. «تفسدوا فى الارض و تقطّعوا ارحامكم»

4- Those who flee from battle, spread corruption, and sever kinship ties are cursed by Allah. «تولّیتم... تفسدوا... تقطّعوا... اولئك الّذین لعنهم اللّه»

5- Deprivation from Divine Mercy is the result of our own actions. «تفسدوا... تقطّعوا... لعنهم اللّه»

6- Failing to utilize one's capabilities is the same as being deprived of them. (He has ears but does not hear the truth; he has eyes but does not see reality.) «لعنهم اللّه فاصمّهم...»

7- The greatest spiritual deprivation is the inability to recognize the truth. «لعنهم اللّه فاصمّهم و اعمى ابصارهم»

8- Illegitimate, unconventional, or unproductive use of God-given talents is a sign of moving away from Divine Mercy. «لعنهم اللّه فاصمّهم و اعمى ابصارهم»

Ayah

أَفَلَا يَتَدَبَّرُونَ ٱلْقُرْءَانَ أَمْ عَلَىٰ قُلُوبٍ أَقْفَالُهَآ (۲٤)
Do they not contemplate the Quran, or are there locks on the hearts? (24)

Key Points

This Ayah, following the previous Ayah, considers the failure to reflect (tadabbur) upon the Quran to be a result of blindness and deafness towards the truths, which afflicts a person due to divine curse (la'nat). In Surah "Sad," Ayah 29, reflection upon the Quran is also enjoined: «كتاب انزلناه الیك مبارك لیدبّروا آیاته و لیتذكّر اولوا الالباب» The Quran is a blessed Book that We sent down to you so that its verses may be reflected upon and those of understanding may take heed.

Messages

1- The Quran is not merely for recitation (tilawah) and observing tajwid (elocution); it is a book of thought and reflection (tadabbur), and recitation must be a prelude to reflection. «أفلا یتدبّرون القرآن»

2- Everyone is commanded to reflect upon the Quran, and reflection is not exclusive to a specific group. «أفلا یتدبّرون القرآن»

3- Those who abandon reflection upon the Quran and treat it superficially are subject to Allah's reproach. «أم على قلوب أقفالها»

4- The condition for benefiting from the sublime concepts of revelation and the Quran is broad-mindedness (sa'at sadr), possessing an open and sound heart, and reflecting upon it. «... أم على قلوب أقفالها»

5- One of the paths to knowledge is the path of the heart (qalb). «أم على قلوب أقفالها»

6- The sign of a sound intellect and heart is reflection upon the verses of the Quran. (The entire Ayah)

Ayah

إِنَّ ٱلَّذِينَ ٱرْتَدُّوا۟ عَلَىٰٓ أَدْبَـٰرِهِم مِّنۢ بَعْدِ مَا تَبَيَّنَ لَهُمُ ٱلْهُدَى ۙ ٱلشَّيْطَـٰنُ سَوَّلَ لَهُمْ وَأَمْلَىٰ لَهُمْ (۲٥)
Indeed those who turned their backs after the guidance had become clear to them, it was Satan who had seduced them, and he had given them [far-flung] hopes. (25)

Key Points

«سوّل لهم» (Sowwala lahum) either means beautifying evils and misrepresenting realities, or it means "facilitation" and making things easy. That is, Satan makes the path of deviation appear easy for man. «أملى » (Amla) either means granting respite or it means providing aid and assistance. The actions of Satan, which are entirely evil, mentioned in the Quran include: whispering/temptation (63), commanding immorality and indecencies (64), beautifying evil deeds (65), creating fear and terror (66) and enmity (67), promising poverty and destitution (68), breaking promises and failing to fulfill them (69), extravagance (70) and hindering the remembrance of Allah (71), discord and division (72), rebellion and disobedience (73), creating doubt and suspicion (74), polytheism and deviation (75), and long and extended hopes (76). Of course, the Quran introduces the way for man to treat and escape Satan in two things: First, that he views him as an enemy, «انّ الشّیطان لكم عدوّ فاتّخذوه عدوّاً» (77). Second, that he seeks refuge in Allah against the evils of Satan. «و امّا ینزغنّك من الشّیطان نزغ فاستعذ باللّه» (78). Otherwise, the external Satan, «الشّیطان سوّل لهم», and the internal Satan, «وكذلك سوّلت لى نفسى» (79), will lead man to ruin and destruction.

63) ناس، 4 و 5. 64) نور، 21. 65) انفال، 48 و انعام، 43. 66) آل عمران، 175. 67) مائده، 91. 68) بقره، 268. 69) ابراهیم، 27. 70) اسراء، 27. 71) نساء، 60. 72) اسراء، 53 و طه، 20. 73) مریم، 44. 74) حج، 3. 75) نمل، 24 ؛ قصص، 15. 76) محمّد، 25. 77) فاطر، 6. 78) اعراف، 200. 79) طه، 96.

Messages

1- Paying attention and listening to the whispers of Satan is among the afflictions of faith and leads to nothing but apostasy and a bad end. «انّ الّذین ارتدّوا»

2- Apostasy and turning away from the truth after it has become clear is a reactionary and blameworthy act. «ارتدّوا على ادبارهم من بعد ما تبیّن...»

3- The path of truth is clear and evident, and awareness and understanding of it brings responsibility. «من بعد ما تبیّن لهم الهُدى»

4- If we do not seriously combat temptations and whispers, awareness and knowledge alone will not suffice. «تبیّن لهم الهُدى الشّیطان سوّل لهم»

5- Beautifying evils and long hopes are the tools of Satan. (Whoever beautifies indecencies possesses the characteristic of Satan.) «الشّیطان سوّل لهم و أملى لهم»

Ayah

ذَٰلِكَ بِأَنَّهُمْ قَالُوا۟ لِلَّذِينَ كَرِهُوا۟ مَا نَزَّلَ ٱللَّهُ سَنُطِيعُكُمْ فِى بَعْضِ ٱلْأَمْرِ ۖ وَٱللَّهُ يَعْلَمُ إِسْرَارَهُمْ (۲٦)
That is because they said to those who loathed what Allah had sent down: ‘We shall obey you in some matters,’ and Allah knows their secret dealings. (26)

Key Points

The hypocrite, to protect himself, seeks out opposing individuals, and even if they do not share common ground in all aspects, he cooperates, and even obeys, to the extent of their shared commonalities. The hypocrites of Medina also had such dealings with the Jews who opposed the Prophet. (80) Perhaps the intent of «للذّین كرهوا ما نزّل اللّه» (for those who disliked what Allah has revealed) are the disbelievers whose names were mentioned at the beginning of the Surah, where He said: «الّذین كفروا... كرهوا ما انزل اللّه» (Those who disbelieved... disliked what Allah has revealed). (81)

80) Tafsir Namuneh. 81) Tafsir Al-Mizan.

Messages

1- Dissatisfied individuals are vulnerable and within the range of opponents' conspiracies. «قالوا للّذین كرهوا... سنطیعكم»

2- Hypocritical individuals promise support, protection, and even obedience to those who are upset and dissatisfied with divine rulings. «سنطیعكم فى بعض الامر»

3- Hypocrites are complicit with disbelievers (Kuffar) and the enemies of Islam. «سنطیعكم فى بعض الامر»

4- A sign of apostasy (Irtidad) is sympathy with the dissatisfied and the disbelievers (Kuffar). «ارتدّوا... سنطیعكم»

5- Following the deviated, even in some matters, is subject to blame and reproach. «سنطیعكم فى بعض الامر»

6- Some act hypocritically to protect their interests and benefits. In some matters, they align with the believers, and in others, they align with the disbelievers, which is blameworthy. (They sometimes hit the horseshoe and sometimes the nail [i.e., they play both sides]). «سنطیعكم فى بعض الامر»

7- The agreements of the hypocrites and the dissatisfied are not public; rather, they are imperceptible and secret. «واللّه یعلم اسرارهم»

8- Let the hypocrites be warned that Allah is aware of their secret deeds and may expose them at any moment. «واللّه یعلم اسرارهم»

9- If a person believes that Allah knows all his secret deeds and mysteries, he will abandon plotting and deal transparently with people. «واللّه یعلم اسرارهم»

Ayahs

فَكَيْفَ إِذَا تَوَفَّتْهُمُ ٱلْمَلَـٰٓئِكَةُ يَضْرِبُونَ وُجُوهَهُمْ وَأَدْبَـٰرَهُمْ (۲۷)
But how will it be [with them] when the angels take them away, striking their faces and their backs?! (27)
ذَٰلِكَ بِأَنَّهُمُ ٱتَّبَعُوا۟ مَآ أَسْخَطَ ٱللَّهَ وَكَرِهُوا۟ رِضْوَٰنَهُۥ فَأَحْبَطَ أَعْمَـٰلَهُمْ (۲۸)
That, because they pursued what displeased Allah, and loathed His pleasure. So He has made their works fail. (28)

Key Points

"Tawaffā" (توفّى) means the complete taking back of something that was given to a person. This word is used in the Qur'an regarding death, because death is, in reality, the taking back of the God-given soul from the body.

In Ayah 50 of Sūrat Al-Anfāl, a similar ayah describes how the disbelievers give up their souls.

Perhaps the punishment of the apostates from the direction of the face: «یضربون وجوههم» (they strike their faces), is the embodiment of turning towards and welcoming Divine wrath «اتّبعوا ما اسخط اللّه» (they followed what angered Allah), and the punishment from the direction of the back: «ادبارهم» (their backs), is the embodiment of turning away (idbār), turning one's back, and disliking Divine pleasure. «و كرهوا رضوانه» (and they disliked His pleasure).

Messages

1- Those who invite others to resist the truth will give up their souls in humiliation and torment. «فكیف اذا توفّتهم...»

2- Death is not annihilation, but the complete taking of the spirit and soul by the angels. «توفّتهم»

3- The punishment of the hypocritical apostates begins from the moment they give up their souls. «توفّتهم الملائكة»

4- Attention to the limited lifespan of this world, the endpoint of conspiracies, and the eventual capture by torment can deter a person from plotting. «فكیف اذا توفّتهم... یضربون...»

5- Enmity towards revelation (Wahy) and the Qur'an leads to Allah's wrath and the nullification of a person's deeds. «ذلك... فاحبط اعمالهم»

6- Although the disbelievers are free to choose their path and method, «اتبعوا, كرهوا» (they followed, they disliked), they have no choice but to accept the effects and consequences thereof. «فاحبط اعمالهم»

7- Sometimes a person sets fire to and destroys the good deeds and what he has sown. «اتّبعوا... كرهوا... فاحبط أعمالهم»

8- Moving along a path where Divine pleasure is absent is futility and deserving of reproach. «اتّبعوا... كرهوا... فاحبط أعمالهم»

Ayahs

أَمْ حَسِبَ ٱلَّذِينَ فِى قُلُوبِهِم مَّرَضٌ أَن لَّن يُخْرِجَ ٱللَّهُ أَضْغَـٰنَهُمْ (۲۹)
Do those in whose hearts is a sickness suppose that Allah will not expose their spite? (29)
وَلَوْ نَشَآءُ لَأَرَيْنَـٰكَهُمْ فَلَعَرَفْتَهُم بِسِيمَـٰهُمْ ۚ وَلَتَعْرِفَنَّهُمْ فِى لَحْنِ ٱلْقَوْلِ ۚ وَٱللَّهُ يَعْلَمُ أَعْمَـٰلَكُمْ (۳٠)
If We wish, We will show them to you so that you recognize them by their mark. Yet you will recognize them by their tone of speech, and Allah knows your deeds. (30)

Key Points

"Ḍaghn" (ضَغن) means intense malice or severe hatred. Imam Ali عليه السلام says: Whoever keeps a secret in his heart, it will become apparent in the course of his speech and on his face. (82) When the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله appointed Imam Ali عليه السلام as his successor at Ghadir Khumm, some people said: Muhammad does not fail to raise the arm of his cousin (due to familial bias and nepotism). Then these verses were revealed. (83)

82) Nahj al-Balaghah, Maxim 26. 83) Tafsir Burhan.

Messages

1- Misusing Allah's attribute of Concealment (Sattāriyyah) is an act of hypocrisy and a sign of a sick heart. «أم حسب»

2- The disgrace of sinners is not exclusive to the Day of Judgment; rather, exposure is possible in this world as well. «ام... ان لن یخرج اللّه اضغانهم»

3- By exposing them, we should disgrace the hypocrites and deprive them of their comfort. «ولو نشاء لاریناكهم...»

4- Envy and malice are factors leading to the sickness of hypocrisy and duplicity. «فى قلوبهم مرض... أضغانهم»

5- A person's inner character and disposition influence his appearance and face, and a person's countenance largely reflects his inner nature. «فلعرفتهم بسیماهم»

6- Not only the content of speech, but also the tone of speech must be polite. The impoliteness of the hypocrites in their speech reveals their inner hypocrisy. «لتعرفنّهم فى لحن القول»

Ayah

وَلَنَبْلُوَنَّكُمْ حَتَّىٰ نَعْلَمَ ٱلْمُجَـٰهِدِينَ مِنكُمْ وَٱلصَّـٰبِرِينَ وَنَبْلُوَا۟ أَخْبَارَكُمْ (۳۱)
We will surely test you until We ascertain those of you who wage jihad and those who are steadfast, and We shall appraise your record. (31)

Key Points

Ibtilā’ (trial/test) sometimes means testing individuals to discover the truth, such as: «اذ ابتلى ابراهیم ربّه» (84) and sometimes it means creating various contexts for the manifestation of inner states, such as this Ayah. (85)

The phrase «حتّى نعلم» (so that We may know) in «نَبلوا اخباركم» (We shall test your reports/deeds) means "We shall make known," derived from the root ‘alam (عَلَم) meaning sign or mark, not from the root ‘ilm (عِلم) meaning knowledge in contrast to ignorance. That is, the Divine test is the sign by which they are identified.

In the previous Ayah, we read that Allah knows our actions, «و اللّه یعلم أعمالكم» (and Allah knows your deeds), and He does not need a test for His own knowledge and awareness. Therefore, the test is so that people perform deeds and, based on those deeds, acquire the deservingness for reward or punishment. Just as we know the occupation of individuals, but we do not pay them wages based merely on knowing their occupation; rather, they must perform work to deserve receiving the wage.

The separation of human beings from one another occurs in stages: In the first stage, people are separated by disbelief (kufr) and faith (īmān); in the second stage, the people of faith are separated by Jihad and fleeing from Jihad; and in the third stage, the Jihad-fighters are separated by greater patience and resistance. Therefore, the Divine tests continue one after another.

From Ayah 16 up to this point, the hypocrites (munāfiqūn) and the sick-hearted have been mentioned repeatedly, and a portrait of them has been drawn, which we summarize below:

  1. Belittling the Prophet's statements. «ماذا قال آنفا» (Ayah 16)
  2. Fear of the command for Jihad. «ذكر فیها القتال رایت الّذین فى قلوبهم مرض ینظرون الیك نظر المغشىّ علیه» (Ayah 20)
  3. Corruption on earth and severing kinship ties. «تفسدوا فى الارض و تقطّعوا ارحامكم» (Ayah 22)
  4. Apostasy and turning away from the truth. «ارتدّوا على ادبارهم من بعد ما تبیّن» (Ayah 25)
  5. They are deceived by the promises of Satan. «الشیطان سوّل لهم و املى لهم» (Ayah 25)
  6. Conspiring and announcing cooperation with other opponents. «سنطیعكم فى بعض الامر» (Ayah 26)
  7. Following deeds that incur Divine wrath. «اتّبعوا ما اسخط اللّه» (Ayah 28)
  8. Displeasure with deeds that lead to Divine pleasure. «كرهوا رضوانه» (Ayah 28)
  9. Malice and resentment. «ام حسب الّذین فى قلوبهم مرض ان لن یخرج اللّه اضغانهم» (Ayah 29)

In response to all this wickedness, Allah also threatens them with sealing their hearts, cursing them and distancing them from His mercy, punishment, striking their faces and backs at the time of death, and the nullification and destruction of their deeds.

84) Al-Baqarah, 124. 85) Mufradāt Rāghib.

Messages

  1. Testing people is an inevitable and universal matter, and one of the Divine traditions. «و لنبلونّكم» (The letters lām and nūn of emphasis indicate its certainty.)
  2. One of the contexts for testing and identifying people is the battlefield. Hypocrites are exposed on the front line. «حتّى نعلم المجاهدین منكم»
  3. In Jihad, patience (sabr) and steadfastness play a very important role. «المجاهدین منكم والصابرین»
  4. Human claims and statements are subjected to Divine testing in the arena of action. «نبلوا اخباركم»
  5. The criterion for Divine evaluation is success in practical tests. «نبلوا اخباركم»

Ayah

إِنَّ ٱلَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا۟ وَصَدُّوا۟ عَن سَبِيلِ ٱللَّهِ وَشَآقُّوا۟ ٱلرَّسُولَ مِنۢ بَعْدِ مَا تَبَيَّنَ لَهُمُ ٱلْهُدَىٰ لَن يَضُرُّوا۟ ٱللَّهَ شَيْـًٔا وَسَيُحْبِطُ أَعْمَـٰلَهُمْ (۳۲)
Indeed those who are faithless and bar from the way of Allah and defy the Apostle after guidance has become clear to them, they will not hurt Allah in the least, and He shall make their works fail. (32)

Key Points

Preventing people from the path of Allah can take many forms, including: distorting divine law, introducing innovations (bid'ah) into the religion, creating doubt in people's beliefs (such as, «أتعلمون أنّ صالحاً مرسل»(86), "How do you know that Salih is a messenger?"), weakening divine leaders (such as calling the Prophets a poet, soothsayer, sorcerer, or madman), threatening people to prevent them from believing, distracting people with trivial and unnecessary matters, creating conflict and strife, enacting restrictive laws to hinder divine works, placing the unqualified in charge of religious affairs, and spreading rumors about worthy individuals.

Sometimes, instead of accepting the truth and striving for his own growth and perfection, man rises up in conflict against the truth and transforms himself into the worst element: - He shows enmity and obstinacy toward Allah. «كفروا... بعد ما تبیّن» - He shows opposition and stubbornness toward the Prophet. «شاقّوا الرسول» - He shows enmity toward people and prevents them from accepting the truth. «صدّوا عن...» - He shows enmity toward himself and causes his own destruction and ruin. «سیحبط اعمالهم»

86) Al-A'raf, 75.

Messages

1- Disbelief (Kufr) is the source of oppression against oneself, society, and the Prophets. «كفروا... صدّوا... شاقّوا الرسول»

2- Allah completes His argument (Hujjah) against people by presenting the paths of guidance and perfection. «تبیّن لهم الهُدى» (Knowing the truth increases man's duty and responsibility.)

3- The disbelief and efforts of the misguided cause no harm whatsoever to Allah. «لن یضروا اللّه»

4- Enmity and obstinacy after gaining awareness lead to the nullification of deeds. «سیحبط اعمالهم»

Ayah

۞ يَـٰٓأَيُّهَا ٱلَّذِينَ ءَامَنُوٓا۟ أَطِيعُوا۟ ٱللَّهَ وَأَطِيعُوا۟ ٱلرَّسُولَ وَلَا تُبْطِلُوٓا۟ أَعْمَـٰلَكُمْ (۳۳)
O you who have faith! Obey Allah and obey the Apostle, and do not render your works void. (33)

Key Points

In the verses (Ayat) and narrations (Riwayat), certain deeds and sayings have been identified as causes for the nullification of actions, including: 1. Reminding the needy of one's favor (imposing obligation) and harming them, which erases the reward of charity. «لاتبطلوا صدقاتكم بالمنّ و الاذى»(87) 2. Disrespect towards Divine leaders. «لاترفعوا اصواتكم فوق صوت النبى أن تحبط اعمالكم»(88) Do not raise your voices above the voice of the Prophet, lest your deeds become nullified. 3. Self-admiration (Ujb) and envy (Hasad). «الهى عبّدنى لك و لاتفسد عبادتى بالعجب»(89) Just as Imam Sadiq عليه السلام said: «انّ الحسد یأكل الایمان كما تأكل النّار الحطب»(90) Envy destroys good deeds just as fire consumes wood. 4. Associating partners with Allah (Shirk). «لئن أشركت لیحبطنّ عملك»(91) If you commit Shirk, your deed will certainly be nullified. 5. Ostentation (Riya), which is a type of Shirk. «الّذین ینفقون أموالهم رئاء النّاس...»(92)

We read in a Hadith that the Messenger of Allah صلى الله علیه وآله said: Whoever says one «سبحان اللّه» (Subhan Allah), a tree is planted for him in Paradise. A person said: Then we (who are constantly engaged in this remembrance) have very many trees there! The Prophet recited the above verse and said: Be careful not to send a fire after that to burn them down.(93)

Regarding the meaning of the phrase «لاتبطلوا أعمالكم» (Do not nullify your deeds), considering the beginning of the verse, three interpretations can be mentioned: A) Preserve your faith and do not become polytheists, otherwise your deeds will be nullified. Because Shirk nullifies deeds. «لئن أشركت لیحبطنّ عملك»(94) B) Do not nullify your deeds by abandoning obedience to the Messenger of Allah. C) Do not nullify your deeds by imposing obligation upon the Prophet (reminding him of your favor). Just as Allah says: «یمنّون علیك أن اسلموا قل لا تمنّوا علىّ اسلامكم»(95)

87) Baqarah, 264. 88) Hujurat, 2. 89) Du'a Makarim al-Akhlaq. 90) Kafi, Vol. 2, p. 306. 91) Zumar, 65. 92) Nisa, 38. 93) Tafsir Nur al-Thaqalayn. 94) Zumar, 65. 95) Hujurat, 17.

Messages

  1. Although the audience of the Quran is the believers, mentioning this title at the beginning of some verses indicates honoring and giving personality to them. «یا أیها الّذین آمنوا أطیعوا اللّه...»

  2. We must observe priorities and hierarchies. Here, the name of Allah comes before the Prophet. «أطیعوا اللّه و أطیعوا الرسول»

  3. The prerequisite of faith is submission and obedience to the commands of Allah and the Messenger. «یا أیّها الّذین آمنوا أطیعوا اللّه و أطیعوا الرسول»

  4. Religious commands are of two types: fixed commands that come from Allah. «أطیعوا اللّه» and variable commands that are issued by the Islamic ruler according to necessities and needs. «أطیعوا الرّسول»

  5. The Prophet of Islam صلى الله علیه وآله is infallible (Ma'sum). The command to obey the Prophet without any condition or restriction is a sign of His infallibility and the necessity of following Him. «أطیعوا الرسول»

  6. Believers should not be arrogant about their faith, as their deeds are at risk. «الّذین آمنوا... لاتبطلوا أعمالكم»

Ayah

إِنَّ ٱلَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا۟ وَصَدُّوا۟ عَن سَبِيلِ ٱللَّهِ ثُمَّ مَاتُوا۟ وَهُمْ كُفَّارٌ فَلَن يَغْفِرَ ٱللَّهُ لَهُمْ (۳٤)
Indeed those who are faithless and bar from the way of Allah and then die faithless, Allah will never forgive them. (34)

Key Points

This Ayah is situated between two Ayahs addressed to the believers, and thus it warns of the danger of apostasy among the believers, which could lead to their disbelief (kufr). This is a danger with a very bad outcome. Although Allah forgives all sins, «انّ اللّه یغفر الذّنوب جمیعاً» (96), and the path of repentance is open for everyone, always, and for every sin until the moment of death, yet those who die in a state of disbelief, or those who apostatize and do not return, or the hypocrites, will not be included in His forgiveness. «ان اللّه لا یغفر ان یشرك به و یغفر ما دون ذلك لمن یشاء» (97), «فلن یغفر اللّه لهم»

96) Zumar, 53. 97) Nisa, 48.

Messages

1- Disbelief (kufr) causes the nullification of a person's past deeds performed during the period of faith. «لاتبطلوا اعمالكم... الّذین كفروا»

2- At the time of death, what is important is whether a person dies as a believer or as a disbeliever. «كفروا... ماتوا و هم كفّار»

3- Disbelief and obstinacy become factors that prevent others from goodness and virtues. «كفروا و صدّوا عن سبیل اللّه»

4- Divine forgiveness is possible until the signs of death are witnessed. «ماتوا و هم كفّار فلن یغفر اللّه لهم»

Ayah

فَلَا تَهِنُوا۟ وَتَدْعُوٓا۟ إِلَى ٱلسَّلْمِ وَأَنتُمُ ٱلْأَعْلَوْنَ وَٱللَّهُ مَعَكُمْ وَلَن يَتِرَكُمْ أَعْمَـٰلَكُمْ (۳٥)
So do not slacken and [do not] call for peace when you have the upper hand and Allah is with you, and He will not stint [the reward of] your works. (35)

Key Points

The phrase «یَتِرَكم» (yatirakum) is derived from watr, meaning deficiency or reduction.

Question: In one place, the Quran states: «و ان جنحوا للسلم فاجنح لها» (98) (If the enemy inclines toward peace, then incline toward it), meaning if the enemy opens its arms for peace, you should also spread your wing and accept the peace proposal. However, this Ayah criticizes peace and states: «فلاتَهنوا و تَدعوا الى السّلم» (So do not weaken and call for peace). What, then, is the duty, and which Ayah should be acted upon?

Answer: Each Ayah addresses a specific aspect. Compromise based on fear, weakness, and panic is undesirable, because this is exactly what the enemies desire: «ودّوا لو تدهن فیُدهنون» (99) (They wish that you would compromise [in religion], so they would compromise [with you]). However, leniency (or conciliation) that stems from a position of strength and compassion for the weak is commendable. Just as walking slowly is sometimes due to physical weakness, and sometimes it is done to accompany children and the elderly who cannot walk fast. Islam states: You must be at a high level in terms of morale, power, and resources, and in that state, if the enemy requests peace, you should accept it.

The intent of Allah's companionship with the believers, «و اللّه معكم» (And Allah is with you), is not the general companionship of Allah, about which He said: «هو معكم این ما كنتم» (100) (He is with you wherever you are), but rather it is a special companionship, which here means the specific Divine honor, victory, and guidance granted to those who strive in His way. (101) As this special companionship is also referred to in other verses: «انّ اللّه لمع المحسنین» (102) (Indeed, Allah is with the doers of good).

When Allah the Almighty addressed the believers saying: «و انتم الاعلون» (And you are the superior ones), it was possible that some believers might become doubtful and hesitant upon seeing the small number of their own forces compared to the multitude, equipment, and power of the enemy. Therefore, Allah immediately said: «واللّه معكم». (103)

98) Anfal, 61. 99) Qalam, 9. 100) Hadid, 4. 101) Tafsir Furqan. 102) Ankabut, 69. 103) Tafsir Kabir Fakhr Razi.

Messages

1- A believer must not allow weakness to enter his soul. «یا أیّها الّذین آمنوا... فلاتهنوا»

2- The firmness and strength of the internal front is the essential condition for victory. «فلاتهنوا...»

3- The proposal for peace and compromise should not originate from the front of faith. «فلاتهنوا و تدعوا الى السلم» (But if the enemies propose it and it is beneficial, it should be accepted. «و ان جنحوا للسلم فاجنح لها»)

4- Give morale to the Muslims and remind them of their own worth. «و أنتم الاعلون واللّه معكم»

5- Divine assistance is always with the striving believers. «واللّه معكم»

6- Allah has guaranteed the reward of the strivers. «و لن یتركم أعمالكم»

Ayah

إِنَّمَا ٱلْحَيَوٰةُ ٱلدُّنْيَا لَعِبٌ وَلَهْوٌ ۚ وَإِن تُؤْمِنُوا۟ وَتَتَّقُوا۟ يُؤْتِكُمْ أُجُورَكُمْ وَلَا يَسْـَٔلْكُمْ أَمْوَٰلَكُمْ (۳٦)
The life of the world is just play and diversion, but if you are faithful and Godwary, He will give you your rewards, and will not ask your wealth [in return] from you. (36)

Key Points

«La'b» (play) is an action that has a trivial motive, and «Lahw» (diversion/amusement) is an action that lacks a rational motive. In the previous Ayah, weakness and compromise with the disbelievers were prohibited. This Ayah seemingly points to the important factor causing weakness, which is attachment to the world, and states: Let the world not deceive you. Joseph (عليه السلام) was a beloved son to his father; a rival in the eyes of his brothers; a slave in the eyes of the travelers who pulled him out of the well; a beloved to Zulaikha; and an interpreter of dreams in the eyes of the prisoners. The world, too, is a means of amusement and gratification in the view of most people, but in the sight of the believers, it is a class for knowing Allah, and everything in it consists of Divine signs (Ayat), a marketplace for trading righteous deeds, a farm for the Hereafter, and a means of acquiring virtue.
