The Divine creation is not based on lahw and la'b (diversion and play), «و ما خلقنا السّموات و الارض و ما بینهما لاعبین» (104) rather, it is man who, due to improper use, turns it into lahw and la'b and a means of adornment, boasting, and accumulation.

104) Ad-Dukhan, 38.

Messages

1- Attachment to the world and worldliness are among the fundamental factors causing weakness and feebleness among believers. «لاتهنوا... انّما الحیاة الدنیا»

2- The world, minus faith (Iman) and piety (Taqwa), is mere play. «لعب و لهو و ان تؤمنوا و تتقوا»

3- Piety (Taqwa) is a result of faith (Iman) and a higher stage. «تؤمنوا و تتقوا»

4- Faith and piety are two factors that remove man's worldly life from futility. «لعب و لهو و ان تؤمنوا و تتّقوا یؤتكم اجوركم»

5- Faith and piety do not contradict the possession of wealth. «تؤمنوا و تتقوا... لایسئلكم اموالكم»

6- The command for spending (Infaq), feeding, Zakat, Khums, and charity (Sadaqah) is for our growth, not for paying the wage of guidance. «لایسئلكم اموالكم»

Ayah

إِن يَسْـَٔلْكُمُوهَا فَيُحْفِكُمْ تَبْخَلُوا۟ وَيُخْرِجْ أَضْغَـٰنَكُمْ (۳۷)
Should He ask it from you and press you, you will be stingy, and He will expose your spite. (37)

Key Points

«Yuhfikum» (یحفكم) is derived from «ihfā'» (احفاء), meaning insistence in demanding and questioning. And «aḍghān» (أضغان) is the plural of «ḍighn» (ضغن), meaning intense malice. The attachment of some people to worldly wealth is so great that even if Allah demands something from them, they harbor malice against Him.

Messages

1- Human characteristics become manifest when encountering divine commands and prohibitions.

«ان یسئلكموها... تبخلوا»

2- Intense attachment to the world and practicing stinginess are subject to criticism and reproach.

«ان یسئلكموها... تبخلوا»

3- Allah has not commanded man regarding matters that are burdensome or cause hardship. «Fayuḥfikum» (فیحفكم) (Divine laws and commands are commensurate with the inherent talent and ability of human beings; we, too, should not establish laws in such a way that people adopt a hostile stance.)

4- The sincerity or malice of friends is revealed at the time of generosity and stinginess. «Yukhrij aḍghānakum» (یخرج أضغانكم)

Ayah

هَـٰٓأَنتُمْ هَـٰٓؤُلَآءِ تُدْعَوْنَ لِتُنفِقُوا۟ فِى سَبِيلِ ٱللَّهِ فَمِنكُم مَّن يَبْخَلُ ۖ وَمَن يَبْخَلْ فَإِنَّمَا يَبْخَلُ عَن نَّفْسِهِۦ ۚ وَٱللَّهُ ٱلْغَنِىُّ وَأَنتُمُ ٱلْفُقَرَآءُ ۚ وَإِن تَتَوَلَّوْا۟ يَسْتَبْدِلْ قَوْمًا غَيْرَكُمْ ثُمَّ لَا يَكُونُوٓا۟ أَمْثَـٰلَكُم (۳۸)
Ah! There you are, being invited to spend in the way of Allah; yet among you there are those who are stingy; and whoever is stingy is stingy only to himself. Allah is the All-sufficient, and you are all-needy, and if you turn away He will replace you with another people, and they will not be like you. (38)

Key Points

Question: In the two previous verses, He said: "لَا يَسْأَلْكُمْ أَمْوَالَكُمْ" (He does not ask you for your wealth), meaning, We do not want anything from your wealth. However, this verse invites people to spend (infaq) and asks something from them. How do we reconcile these verses?

Answer: The intent of "لَا يَسْأَلْكُمْ أَمْوَالَكُمْ" is that We do not ask anything from you in return for your guidance and faith, nor do We have any need for you for Our own sake. However, the invitation to spend (infaq) in this verse is for the purpose of establishing social justice, unity, philanthropy, and removing the resentments, grudges, and discriminations which, if left unresolved, will turn class differences into a complex, an explosion, and a desire for revenge. The invitation to spend is for the preservation of the human social order itself.

Therefore, Allah says: whoever is stingy will only be stingy against his own soul, and Allah is Rich (Ghani) and Self-Sufficient in every respect.

We read in the commentaries (tafasir) that the Noble Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله, in response to someone who asked: Who are those other people who will not be stingy? The Prophet placed His hand on Salman’s shoulder and said: The reference is to the people of Salman (the people of Iran). I swear by Him in Whose Hand is my soul, if faith were tied to the Pleiades (Thurayya), a group of Persians would surely attain it. (105)

In the method of education (tarbiyah), certain stages must be followed: A) Alerting the audience. "هَا أَنْتُمْ" B) Request and invitation. "تُدْعَوْنَ..." C) Justification and reasoning. "مَن يَبْخَلْ فَإِنَّمَا يَبْخَلُ عَنْ نَفْسِهِ" D) Repetition and decisiveness. "وَاللَّهُ الْغَنِيُّ وَأَنْتُمُ الْفُقَرَاءُ" E) Completion of the argument (ultimatum) and threat. "وَإِنْ تَتَوَلَّوْا يَسْتَبْدِلْ..."

(105) Tafasir Majma' al-Bayan, Al-Mizan, Namuneh, Kabir Fakhr Razi, Maraghi, Ruh al-Ma'ani, and Shabbar.

Messages

  1. In propagation (tabligh), sometimes the audience must be sensitized to listen. "هَا أَنْتُمْ..."
  2. Spend (infaq) with your own hands while you can, for the inheritors will do nothing. "لِتُنْفِقُوا"
  3. What is subject to the Divine invitation is sincere action. "لِتُنْفِقُوا فِي سَبِيلِ اللَّهِ"
  4. People must be invited to spend and help others, but they must be left free to take action based on their own thought and choice. "فَمِنْكُمْ مَنْ يَبْخَلُ"
  5. The one who is stingy thinks he has deprived people of his wealth, while in reality, he has deprived himself of popularity, honor, and proximity to Allah. "فَإِنَّمَا يَبْخَلُ عَنْ نَفْسِهِ"
  6. Stinginess relates to the individual's spirit; it is not necessarily linked to being wealthy. How many people possess capital yet are not stingy. "فَمِنْكُمْ مَنْ يَبْخَلُ"
  7. The invitation to spend is not a sign of Allah's need. "وَاللَّهُ الْغَنِيُّ"
  8. In propagation, we should not let the audience become arrogant. "تُدْعَوْنَ لِتُنْفِقُوا... أَنْتُمُ الْفُقَرَاءُ" (Alongside the invitation to spend, it reminds them of God's Self-Sufficiency and man's poverty and need.)
  9. In propagation and invitation, flattery of the people should be avoided, and the dignity of Divine laws must be preserved. "تُدْعَوْنَ لِتُنْفِقُوا... وَإِنْ تَتَوَلَّوْا يَسْتَبْدِلْ"
  10. Allah's laws are not suspended, even if you fail to implement them. "إِنْ تَتَوَلَّوْا يَسْتَبْدِلْ قَوْمًا غَيْرَكُمْ"
  11. Just as past history is a lesson, foresight can also be constructive. "يَسْتَبْدِلْ قَوْمًا غَيْرَكُمْ"
  12. Abandoning spending (infaq) is so dangerous that Allah might uproot and destroy a nation or a regime. "وَإِنْ تَتَوَلَّوْا يَسْتَبْدِلْ..."
  13. For education and encouragement, sometimes the leverage of threat must be used. "وَإِنْ تَتَوَلَّوْا يَسْتَبْدِلْ"
  14. The earth will never remain empty of righteous individuals. "يَسْتَبْدِلْ قَوْمًا غَيْرَكُمْ ثُمَّ لَا يَكُونُوا أَمْثَالَكُمْ" (It is the Divine tradition that the earth should not be empty of a Proof/Hujjah.)
  15. What matters to Allah are obedient servants; it is not about "me and you." "قَوْمًا غَيْرَكُمْ ثُمَّ لَا يَكُونُوا أَمْثَالَكُمْ" (The Divine religion is not dependent on any specific group or nation for its survival and continuity.)
